facts: Earth's oceans contain roughly 1.35 billion cubic kilometers of water.
The largest known water reservoir, found around a quasar, holds 140 TRILLION times all of Earth's water.
Water vapor, ice, and even liquid water are common across the cosmos: on comets, moons like Europa and Enceladus, and in exoplanet atmospheres.
idea.payoff: Water, the essential molecule for life, is one of the most abundant compounds in the entire universe!
idea.title: Universe's MEGA-OCEANS?! 🌊🤯

scenes.visual: A vibrant, rotating Earth centered, with a deep blue ocean prominent, against a dark starry background.

scenes.text: Earth's oceans: 1.35 billion km³ of water.
scenes.visual: Close-up of Earth's oceans, with an animated overlay highlighting the vast volume of water within, perhaps a translucent layer or flowing lines.

scenes.text: Largest known: Quasar reservoir. 140 TRILLIONx Earth's water!
scenes.visual: Abstract visualization of a distant, intensely bright quasar, with energy waves emanating. A numerical counter or large '140 TRILLION' graphic appears and expands.
A quick montage: a glowing comet streaking through space, then a close-up of Europa's icy cracked surface, finally a stylized exoplanet with swirling atmospheric bands.

scenes.text: Water, essential for life, is abundant across the ENTIRE UNIVERSE!
scenes.visual: A majestic, wide shot of a swirling galaxy or a vibrant nebula, with countless stars and glowing gas. Subtle H2O molecules could shimmer and drift across the screen.
